basics.name: Paul D. Martin, Ph.D.
basics.summary: Chief Scientist at Harbor Experts and Lecturer at Johns Hopkins University. Expert witness in software security, embedded systems, and reverse engineering with testimony experience in federal and state courts, the ITC, and inter partes reviews.

publications.name: Sentinel: Secure Mode Profiling and Enforcement for Embedded Systems
publications.publisher: Proc. ACM/IEEE International Conference on Internet-of-Things Design and Implementation (IoTDI '18)
publications.summary: Hardware-based security system for IoT-class embedded devices providing control-flow integrity through runtime profiling and enforcement.

publications.name: Applications of Secure Location Sensing in Healthcare
publications.publisher: Proc. ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, and Health Informatics (BCB '16)
publications.summary: Secure indoor location tracking system using unspoofable Bluetooth Low Energy beacons for healthcare authentication.

publications.name: KBID: Kerberos Bracelet Identification
publications.publisher: Proc. Financial Cryptography and Data Security (FC '16)
publications.summary: Authentication bracelet using Kerberos tickets transmitted over the wearer's skin for password-free authentication.

publications.name: Classifying Network Protocol Implementation Versions: An OpenSSL Case Study
publications.summary: Techniques for fingerprinting and classifying specific versions of network protocol implementations.

publications.name: Enforcing Minimum Necessary Access in Healthcare Through Integrated Audit and Access Control
publications.summary: System for enforcing minimum necessary access in healthcare through large-scale audit log analysis and access control integration.

teaching.description: Highest-rated course in the CS department during its session.
